scientific_justification.what_is_being_claimed: The observed I–V characteristic may be interpreted as thermionic emission if current magnitude and slope indicate thermally activated carrier transport across an energy barrier.

mechanism_proposals.interpretation_note: Thermionic emission is conditionally proposed when current magnitude and slope are consistent with thermally activated carrier transport over an energy barrier.

scientific_justification.important_note: This justification proposes thermionic emission only under conditions consistent with thermally activated transport behavior.
